title: Specify rule frontmatter
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter block at the top of a rule markdown file. The schema applies only to the machine-readable frontmatter between the opening and closing `---` delimiters; Markdown body validation, including the required `## Rule` heading and duplicate-id checks across the first-party rule set, is handled by `specify lint framework`.

id.description: Stable rule identifier. V1 reserves UNI, SRC, FRAME, RUST, IFACE, SEC, OMNIA, VECTIS, and ORG namespaces with three-digit numeric suffixes. SRC namespaces shared source-adapter overlays; FRAME is reserved for framework declarative checks and is not used in consumer adapter codex.

severity.description: Default review severity for findings associated with this rule.

trigger.description: One-sentence condition that tells a generator or reviewer when the rule matters.

applicability.description: Small V1 applicability filter. These fields narrow where a rule is likely relevant; they are not suppressions, exceptions, or policy overrides.

languages.description: Lowercase language or format tokens such as `rust`, `typescript`, or `json-schema`.

artifacts.description: Closed artifact category tokens. Consumer-side tokens (`code`, `tests`, `contracts`, `specs`, `design`, `tasks`) compose with framework-side tokens (`skill`, `adapter`, `brief`, `reference`, `codex`, `rfc`, `doc`).

paths.description: Relative path or glob patterns. Absolute paths, URI strings, and `..` segments are rejected.

lint_mode.description: How this rule is expected to be linted. The lint runner decides execution; this field only classifies the rule.

rule_hints.description: Optional hints for future deterministic checks. V1 validates only the hint shape, not regex syntax or tool availability.

kind.description: Kind of deterministic signal the hint describes. Every kind in the enum is executed by the `specify lint` hint interpreter, which carries one `eval/<kind>.rs` interpreter module per `const`; no kind is reserved. Adding a new kind requires landing its interpreter in the same change.

value.description: Hint payload, interpreted by a future validator or review tool.

description.description: Optional human explanation for the hint.

config.description: Per-kind configuration. Phase 1: `regex` hints may carry regexHintConfig.

references.description: External or repository-local reference material that helps apply the rule.

label.description: Short display label for the reference.

deprecated.description: When present, marks this rule as deprecated while preserving the stable id for historical citations.

reason.description: Human-readable reason the rule is deprecated.

replaced_by.description: Replacement rule id, when there is a direct successor.

negative-match.description: When true, emit one finding per line where the pattern does not match.

capture-group.description: 1-based capture group index to parse as an integer for threshold comparison.

capture-op.description: Comparison operator applied to the captured integer.

suffix-must-not-start-with.description: Per-match filter: skip matches whose remainder starts with this literal prefix.

slash-skill-positional.description: When true, apply CORE-023 slash-skill positional semantics instead of matching `value` as a regex.

join-backslash-continuations.description: When true with `slash-skill-positional`, join backslash- and indent-continued rows before scanning.

relativePathPattern.description: Relative path or glob. A leading `!` marks an exclusion path-pattern hint (RFC-31 Phase 2).
